title: attendance — retention windows
description: Attendance records retain 7 years post-session (10 years Enterprise via attendance_extended_retention entitlement) for federation + tax + regulatory-records compliance. Absence records retain 3 years. Late arrivals co-terminous with parent AttendanceRecord. Attendance policies retain while active + 7 years post-archive. Records under regulator hold survive TenantErased as compliance archive rows.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 17 (retention exception for regulatory records)
US Department of Education (youth program records)
state youth sports SafeSport (multi-year verification trails)
SOC 2 Type II CC7.2 (attendance events audit-critical)

cold.days: 2555 (7 years post session.ends_at); 3650 (10 years Enterprise via attendance_extended_retention)

purge.trigger: > 7 years post session.ends_at (or 10 years Enterprise) AND no regulator hold
purge.notes: Federation regulators may require multi-year lookback for age-eligibility disputes. Enterprise extends to 10 years for litigation-hold scenarios.

purge.trigger: > 7 years past deactivation AND no LateArrival references remain
> 3 years past session.ends_at AND no linked no-show fee invoice active
purge.notes: Anti-discrimination audit inspection may require pattern retention. Compliance module may extend on demand.
Fraud correlation signals retain 1 year — enough for cross-tenant pattern analysis without extending PII retention. Records themselves survive; only these columns are nulled.

purge.action: S3 object deletion cascaded via AttendanceRecord hard-delete

cross_reference: TenantErased cascades hard-delete of every attendance / policy / absence / late-arrival row. Compliance archive rows survive per compliance module for regulator holds.
Session archive (soft-delete) retains attendance data indefinitely; only Session hard-delete post-retention (7 years) triggers attendance hard-purge.
Membership hard-delete cascades: attendance records with consumed_pass_id pointing at that membership's Passes retain (consumed_pass_id RESTRICTs the pass hard-delete).
